regulatory_milestone_updates.milestone_description: High-risk AI system obligations originally due August 2026
regulatory_milestone_updates.evidence: Digital Omnibus negotiations propose delay to December 2027 or August 2028 due to unavailability of harmonized standards; political agreement needed before June 2026

regulatory_milestone_updates.milestone_description: Code of Practice on AI-generated content transparency
regulatory_milestone_updates.evidence: Expected finalization May-June 2026 ahead of August 2026 enforceability; working group meetings through May 2026

risk_pattern_detections.observation: EU Digital Omnibus negotiations continue with proposed delays for high-risk AI obligations
risk_pattern_detections.significance: Reinforces EU/US schism and implementation delays benefiting incumbents

risk_pattern_detections.observation: Digital Omnibus delay justified by CEN/CENELEC standards unavailability
risk_pattern_detections.significance: Confirms absence of harmonized standards ahead of August 2026 deadlines

risk_pattern_detections.observation: EU Code of Practice on AI-generated content transparency nearing finalization; French investigation into Grok deepfakes; proposed nudifier ban
risk_pattern_detections.significance: Legislative responses to AI-enabled synthetic media in FIMI contexts

risk_pattern_detections.observation: French authorities investigate Grok for non-consensual deepfake generation; EU proposes nudifier ban in Digital Omnibus
risk_pattern_detections.significance: Ongoing misuse cases prompting targeted prohibitions

risk_pattern_detections.observation: xAI signs partial GPAI Code of Practice (safety chapter only)
risk_pattern_detections.significance: Bifurcated compliance pathways among GPAI providers

lab_posture_changes.observation: Released GPT-5.5 with agentic capabilities on April 23; amended Microsoft partnership for multi-cloud; AWS partnership announced
lab_posture_changes.significance: Rapid capability escalation and infrastructure diversification reducing vendor lock-in

lab_posture_changes.observation: Signed only Safety and Security chapter of EU GPAI Code of Practice
lab_posture_changes.significance: Selective EU compliance creating alternative transparency pathways

cross_monitor_escalation_flags.summary: OpenAI multi-cloud strategy (Microsoft amendment + AWS partnership) diversifies infrastructure away from single provider
EU Code of Practice for AI-generated content transparency expected May-June 2026 addresses deepfake labeling
French Grok deepfake investigation and EU nudifier ban proposal highlight synthetic media misuse risks

contested_findings.contradiction: Grok deepfake investigation reported by single Tier 2 source without primary French authority confirmation
Nudifier ban proposal reported by single Tier 2 source without official EU legislative text

analyst_briefing: No capability tier reviews possible due to absent capability_registry. EU AI Act faces slippage on high-risk obligations via Digital Omnibus negotiations (AGM-2026-05-02-006), justified by standards vacuum, maintaining Governance Fragmentation and Standards Vacuum risks at HIGH. Disinfo Velocity matched by imminent AI content transparency Code (AGM-2026-05-02-007) and deepfake responses. AI-Generated Harm persists with Grok investigation (AGM-2026-05-02-008, contested) and nudifier ban proposal (AGM-2026-05-02-009, contested). OpenAI demonstrates increased capability tempo with GPT-5.5 agentic release and multi-cloud pivot (AGM-2026-05-02-001-003); xAI adopts partial EU GPAI safety compliance (AGM-2026-05-02-010). ESA flags on OpenAI infrastructure diversification (MEDIUM); FCW/WDM on deepfake measures (MEDIUM/LOW). Two Tier 2 findings require human review for primary source verification. EU industrial policy advances on track via funding and Action Plan milestones.
